narrative:
Karnataka High Court heard 734 of 1,657 listed matters on 30 April, an overall hearing rate of **44.3%**. That is higher than the available recent benchmarks of **40.3%** on 28 April, **42.0%** on 25 April, and **35.0%** on 27 April, though today’s board was also lighter than those days. The day’s disposal was driven most by the **Orders** stage, which accounted for **335 heard matters** and converted at **48.8%**, up from **39.3%** on 28 April and **28.4%** on 27 April. **Preliminary Hearing** was the strongest stage by conversion at **57.3%** (94 of 164), while **Further Hearing** was the weakest at **23.7%** (42 of 177). **Final Hearing** also stayed soft at **38.4%**.

Judge-wise, the strongest high-volume performances came from **Anant Ramanath Hegde** with **44 heard out of 59 listed**, **E.S. Indiresh** with **46 of 71**, **R. Devdas** with **43 of 64**, **K. Manmadha Rao** with **43 of 69**, and **Rajesh Rai K** with **32 of 41**. **S.R. Krishna Kumar** also posted a strong primary board at **38 of 46**. On the lower-conversion side, **H.T. Narendra Prasad** heard **39 of 190**, **S. Sunil Dutt Yadav**’s main list moved **17 of 125**, and **M.I. Arun** showed **0 of 58**.

By case type, **WP** remained the dominant load at **821 scheduled cases**, nearly half the docket, but converted at only **36.1%**; that left writ matters as the biggest source of pending carryover. In contrast, criminal categories moved better: **CRL.A** converted at **69.4%**, **CRL.RP** at **68.2%**, and **CRL.P** at **63.3%**. That is a clear improvement for **CRL.P** over **27.4%** on 28 April and **28.8%** on 27 April, although on much smaller volume today. **MFA** stayed comparatively steady at **52.1%**, close to prior-day patterns.
